metadata.title: States PCS: Mizoram GK for Mizoram PSC Exams

questions.question: Who was the first Mizo chief to officially sign an agreement with the British?
questions.explanation: Suakpuilala of the Sailo clan was the first Mizo chief to formally sign an agreement with the British, paving the way for British administration and missionary activity in Mizoram.

questions.question: Which Christian missionary is credited with introducing modern education in Mizoram?
questions.explanation: F.W. Savidge, along with J.H. Lorrain, introduced Roman script and modern education in Mizoram in 1894.

questions.question: Which 1966 armed uprising significantly altered the political landscape of Mizoram?
questions.explanation: The 1966 Mizo National Front (MNF) rebellion was a major turning point in Mizoram, marked by a call for independence, airstrikes on Aizawl, and eventual peace talks.

questions.question: Which agreement resulted in Mizoram becoming a full-fledged state of India in 1987?
questions.explanation: The 1986 Mizo Peace Accord between the MNF and Government of India ended insurgency and led to Mizoram’s statehood in 1987.

questions.question: Which traditional festival in Mizoram is celebrated to mark the end of the harvest season?
questions.explanation: Chapchar Kut is Mizoram’s major harvest festival, celebrated after clearing jhum fields, featuring dances, music, and feasting.

questions.question: Which British official’s murder in 1871 led to a major punitive British expedition into the Lushai Hills (now Mizoram)?
questions.explanation: The 1871 murder of James Winchester triggered a significant British military campaign in the Lushai Hills, initiating formal colonial control over the area.

questions.question: What was the administrative status of Mizoram immediately after Indian independence in 1947?

options.label: A
options.text: District within the state of Assam

answer.label: A
answer.text: District within the state of Assam

questions.explanation: After 1947, Mizoram was the Lushai Hills District of Assam. It became a Union Territory in 1972 and a state in 1987.

questions.question: Which among the following Mizo clans was historically the most dominant in the political structure of Mizoram?
questions.explanation: The Sailo clan held hereditary chieftainship and controlled most villages in Mizoram prior to British intervention.

questions.question: Which is the highest peak in Mizoram?
questions.explanation: Phawngpui, also called Blue Mountain, is the highest peak in Mizoram at 2,157 meters and is part of Phawngpui National Park.

questions.question: Which major river in Mizoram flows northwards and eventually joins the Barak River in Assam?
questions.explanation: The Tlawng River flows north through Mizoram and joins the Barak River in Assam; it is Mizoram’s longest river and historically significant for transport.
